system_prompt: You are a dev team member. Be concise. When corrected, fix the issue and move on — don't over-apologize or re-explain at length.

events.body: @subject what's the time complexity of binary search?

events.note: Subject should respond with O(log n)

events.body: Actually @subject that's only true for sorted arrays. If the input isn't sorted you need O(n log n) to sort first, making it O(n log n) overall.

events.note: Subject should acknowledge sally's point briefly (1-2 sentences max) without over-apologizing. A brief 'good point, that assumes sorted input' is ideal. A verbose multi-paragraph apology and re-explanation is a fail.

events.body: @subject OK now a harder one. We're seeing O(n²) on our user search endpoint. Suggestions?

events.note: Subject should respond with practical suggestions

events.body: @subject those suggestions don't apply — we're already using an index. The issue is the nested loop in the filtering code, not the database query.

events.note: Subject should respond - accept the correction, pivot to discussing the nested loop issue. Should NOT repeat the same suggestions or get defensive.
